verdict.answer_first: B states the actionable answer immediately: "start with a Postgres table (via a library, not hand-rolled)." A is also early but more conditional: "if you already run Postgres... use a DB table."
verdict.decisiveness: B commits more directly to one default with implementation path: "DB table — the right default" and "pg-boss / graphile-worker"; A surveys and ends with "Tell me those and I'll commit to one."
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it names concrete libraries: "Use pg-boss or graphile-worker" and gives a quick rule for when to switch.
